query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "prorl: effective reinforcement learning for proactive recommendation v" computer science
rationale: This paper addresses the core mechanism of Reinforcement Learning (RL) and Inverse RL (IRL) within the domain of recommendation-like planning tasks (Wikispeedia), satisfying criteria (a) for same-mechanism evidence and (b) for measuring the independent variable (RL/IRL algorithms) in a relevant domain. Although it focuses on hierarchical strategies rather than the specific "Proactive" aspect of the user's query, it provides the foundational methodological context (BIHRL vs. standard BIRL) that a
